methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $104M total raised proxy.

methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Alice & Bob is a Series B quantum computing company founded by Theau Peronnin and Raphael Lescanne, having raised $104M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a sector comp universe of 11 peers across quantum computing: 3 public companies (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private firms including Q-CTRL, IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, and D-Wave Quantum.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable triangulation method. Using total capital raised ($104M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement yields an implied valuation of $198M. This method directly reflects risk-stage transition patterns observed across the comp set.

Peer-median comparison was not applied due to insufficient narrow comparables: while 7 same-sector peers exist, only 1 operates at the identical stage with no adjacent-stage or similar-tier datapoints available (3+ required for reliability). Broader-sector medians would introduce unwarranted noise given quantum computing's heterogeneity in business model and maturity.

The triangulated valuation range is $168M–$227M, with a median of $198M. This range carries meaningful uncertainty around stage-comparable growth rates and the relevance of earlier-stage and later-stage peers in the sparse quantum computing public market. Refinement would benefit from additional Series B or late-stage quantum hardware or software comparables.
